articles.title: OpenClaw and Claude can put your AI-generated podcasts in Spotify
articles.description:
Save to Spotify is a new command-line tool designed specifically for AI agents like OpenClaw, Claude Code, or OpenAI Codex. If you're the kind of person who collects research on a topic, then feeds it through their AI of choice to create audio summaries and personal podcasts, this lets you save them right alongside the latest episode of The Vergecast and Welcome to Night Vale on Spotify.
To set it up, you need to download and install the Save to Spotify CLI from GitHub. Then you just prompt your AI agent as normal, but tack on "and save to Spotify," and it should show up right in your podcast feed. In the blog post announcing the feature, S …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Turning up the volume on macrophage-driven immune responses

articles.title: Can positive experiences help break the cycle of abuse?
articles.description: Our childhood experiences create ripple effects across our lives and with those whom we encounter. Adverse childhood experiences, or ACEs, have been linked to depression and dementia, and even to perpetuating abuse. The intergenerational transmission of abuse is well-known to affect the children of victims, and recent evidence has also connected a high number of ACEs to an increased risk of abusing elders, indicating broad impacts on later-life relationships and violent behavior.

articles.title: Google’s taking a big swing at AI health with the Fitbit Air
articles.description:
The Fitbit Air can be preordered today and will be available starting May 26th. | Image: Google	

It's a Whoop dupe. That was my first thought when I saw the new $99 Google Fitbit Air. You can hardly blame me. The band is screenless with a metallic fabric clasp. My eyes flickered between the Fitbit Air and my wrist, where I'm wearing a Whoop MG. Was I not seeing double?
But as my press briefing went on, my opinion started changing. The Air is sort of like the OG Fitbits that Whoop then duped once Fitbit went all in on smartwatches. Think back to 2012, when the Fitbit One could clip to your pants, be turned into a pendant, or dangle from a keychain. That device was mostly a pedometer, whereas the Air is more of a modern, modular sensor t …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Ploopy’s new mouse makes the ThinkPad’s iconic TrackPoint portable
articles.description:
The Bean isn’t wireless so you’ll need to pair it with a USB cable. | Image: Ploopy	

Ploopy has announced another open source mouse alternative that skips touchpads and trackballs. While the new Ploopy Bean looks like a tiny travel-friendly mouse, you don't need to move it around. Instead, surrounded by four buttons is a red pointing stick similar to the TrackPoint that IBM included on its ThinkPad laptops long before Lenovo took over the brand.
The Canadian company only shares pricing in its local currency but the Bean is available for preorder now through Ploopy's website for $69.99, or around US $51. Beneath a pre-assembled 3D-printed enclosure that lets you print your own replacement parts is a highly-sensitive Texas In …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Study identifies post-extubation pneumonia as a distinct condition after surgery and determines key risk factors
articles.description: A Hiroshima University study of more than 31,000 patients found that pneumonia occurred more often after breathing tubes were removed than during ventilation, with most cases developing within a 1–2 week window after surgery. The findings suggest this under-recognized condition may be a distinct clinical entity linked to swallowing dysfunction and that early assessment and intervention, including identification of high-risk patients, may be key to prevention and improved outcomes.

articles.title: Rat Poison In Watermelon Killed Mumbai Family, Forensic Analysis Reveals
articles.description: Forensic tests have revealed that all four of them died because of a lethal rodenticide.

articles.title: Rat poison ingredient caused deaths of four members of Mumbai family, not watermelon consumption: Official
articles.description: Tragedy struck a South Mumbai family as four members, including two daughters, succumbed to zinc phosphide poisoning, not watermelon as initially suspected. The family experienced severe vomiting and loose motions after consuming the chemical, commonly found in rat poison. Investigations are ongoing, with forensic reports expected to confirm the cause of death.

articles.title: Centre has given positive response to demand for widening NH 150A, says MP
articles.description: The road development works will significantly boost the social and economic development of Yadgir and Raichur districts: Kumar Naik

articles.title: Exhibit at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Get in front of 10,000 decision-makers before space runs out
articles.description: If startup visibility, traction, and real deals matter, you should be on the TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 exhibit floor. Get your 6' exhibit table before your competitor does.

articles.title: Angry Birds and FIFA International Soccer join the World Video Game Hall of Fame
articles.description: Silent Hill and Dragon Quest round out the class of 2026.

articles.title: Spotify's AI-powered personal DJ expands to more languages and countries
articles.description: Premium users in more than 75 markets will now have access to it.

articles.title: Nellore Red Cross chairman receives award for outstanding community service
Demographic imbalance in India needs more attention than population control, says RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat
articles.description: With to Common Civil Code, Mr. Bhagwat referred to the introduction of the law in a few States in India, including Uttarakhand, and expressed optimism that the Uniform Civil Code will gradually be implemented across India

topStories.title: The balcony solar boom is coming to the US
topStories.description: Dozens of US states are considering legislation to allow people to install plug-in solar systems, often called balcony solar. These small arrays require little to no setup and could help cut emissions and power bills. Balcony solar is already popular in Europe, and proponents say that the systems could make solar power more accessible for…

topStories.description: A slew of new civil lawsuits allege UPS and its partners failed their responsibility to make sure a plane they put in the air was safe to fly.

topStories.title: Purported Jeffrey Epstein suicide note had echoes of messages he had sent earlier
topStories.description: The note contains an apparent reference to a line from a 1931 Little Rascals short film that Epstein had used in at least two email messages.

dailyNews.title: A blueprint for using AI to strengthen democracy
dailyNews.description: Every few centuries, changes in how information moves reshape how societies govern themselves. The printing press spread vernacular literacy, helping give rise to the Reformation and, eventually, representative government. The telegraph made it possible to administer vast nations like the US, accelerating the growth of the modern bureaucratic state. Broadcast media created shared national audiences,…

dailyNews.title: Palestinians Stream Back to Northern Gaza on Foot
dailyNews.description: Israel allowed displaced Gazans to begin crossing a military zone that bisects the enclave after a deadlock over hostage releases was broken.

dailyNews.title: Leading China Property Developer Reports Huge loss, in Sign of Widening Real-Estate Woes
dailyNews.description: Troubles at Vanke raise questions about the continued spread of the property crisis and whether the Chinese state will step in.

dailyNews.title: Freed Israeli Hostages Still Had Shrapnel in Their Bodies From Oct. 7 Attack
dailyNews.description: Some of the women were held alone for extended periods and spent eight months in tunnels, an Israeli medical official said.

dailyNews.title: Suspected Sabotage of Deep-Sea Cable Triggers First NATO-Led Response
dailyNews.description: The alliance mounted its first coordinated response to a suspected sabotage campaign against critical infrastructure after another cable was severed in the Baltic Sea.

dailyNews.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City
dailyNews.description: Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

dailyNews.title: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
dailyNews.description: Attacks by powerful militias against civilians reflect the state’s inaction eight years after a peace accord removed a powerful rebel group from the field.

dailyNews.title: Rubio holds ‘constructive’ meeting with Pope Leo after Trump sends hard-line Iran message to Vatican
dailyNews.description: Rubio and Pope Leo held a private Vatican meeting to ease tensions between the Trump administration and the pontiff over the U.S.-Iran conflict.

dailyNews.title: Seattle mayor refuses to grade herself as crime, homelessness surge
dailyNews.description: Seattle Mayor Katie Wilson refused to grade herself amid a growing homelessness and crime crisis, saying she is always striving to do better.

dailyNews.title: Taylor Sheridan's hit CIA/military series gets major update ahead of new season premiere
dailyNews.description: "Lioness" season 3 could premiere by end of summer, star Michael Kelly says, after confirming Taylor Sheridan's hit CIA series has finished production.

dailyNews.title: Mamdani's latest tax pledge 'makes no sense' and will only exacerbate Florida 'exodus': former mayor
dailyNews.description: Republican congressional candidate Scott Singer predicts continued business exodus from NYC to Florida amid Mayor Mamdani's tax-the-rich policies.

topStories.description: Rubio and Pope Leo held a private Vatican meeting to ease tensions between the Trump administration and the pontiff over the U.S.-Iran conflict.

topStories.title: Judge Says F.B.I. Can Keep 2020 Election Records Seized From Georgia
topStories.description: The ruling is the latest in a saga driven by President Trump’s desire for redemption in the state, which he lost in 2020. The county is likely to appeal.

topStories.title: Jack Smith Calls the Justice Dept. ‘Corrupted’ by Trump and His Allies
topStories.description: At a private event in Washington last month, Mr. Smith, the former special counsel, accused Justice Department leaders of targeting people for prosecution to please and impress the president.

topStories.title: Trump says war to end quickly as Iran claims control of Strait of Hormuz
topStories.description: As President Trump again voices optimism that Iran will "make a deal" to end the war, Tehran declares itself the regulator of Strait of Hormuz shipping.

topStories.title: Rubio meeting with Pope Leo after tense exchanges between Trump and the first U.S.-born pontiff
topStories.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is meeting with Pope Leo at the Vatican. It follows criticism by President Trump of Pope Leo and pushback by the first U.S.-born pontiff. Ramy Inocencio reports.

topStories.title: Rubio visits the Vatican and meets with Pope Leo following Trump feud
topStories.description: State Secretary Marco Rubio met with Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican following the tense moments involving President Trump, the pontiff and the Iran war. CBS News' Ramy Inocencio reports.

topStories.title: Indiana Republicans who lost their jobs after bucking Trump have ‘zero regrets’
topStories.description: Three state senators who were defeated in Tuesday’s primary told NBC News they haven’t changed their minds about the redistricting vote that drew Trump’s ire.

topStories.title: Marco Rubio Meets With Pope Leo XIV Over Iran War and Cuba
topStories.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is meeting with Pope Leo XIV in the Apostolic Palace at the Vatican and the State Department says the conversations will include the situation in the Middle East and mutual interests in the Western Hemisphere. It comes after a series of tense exchanges between President Donald Trump and the pontiff over the Iran war. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

topStories.title: Indiana primaries show the political cost of crossing Trump: ANALYSIS
topStories.description: The message from Indiana’s Republican primaries was hard to miss: if you cross Trump -- even in a state legislative race -- he’s willing to come after you politically.

topStories.title: Trump asks judges to pause E. Jean Carroll ruling so he can appeal case to SCOTUS
topStories.description: President Trump has asked a court to pause its ruling rejecting his challenge to E. Jean Carroll's defamation suit so he can appeal the case to the Supreme Court.

topStories.description: Republican lawmakers in Tennessee are poised to vote on a plan to reshape a majority-Black congressional district

dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
dailyNews.description: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.

dailyNews.title: Rubio visits Rome aiming to repair White House ties with Pope Leo
dailyNews.description: Relations between the United States and the Vatican are at a low point over President Donald Trump’s attacks on Leo, who is a leading critic of the war in Iran.

dailyNews.title: Undeterred by polls and courts, Trump pushes ballroom, honors donors
dailyNews.description: Amid the war with Iran, surging gas prices and backlash to his immigration policies, the president continues to dedicate extensive time to his signature project.

dailyNews.title: Federal workers had their annual award show. Many were too afraid to show up.
dailyNews.description: A year after the U.S. DOGE Service dramatically slashed the civil service, government workers are still spooked about getting on the Trump administration’s bad side.

dailyNews.description: The unusual White House scene highlighted President Donald Trump’s diverse set of priorities as he juggles a war with event planning.

dailyNews.title: Trump lashes out at Pope Leo again ahead of Rubio trip to Rome
dailyNews.description: The criticism of the pontiff’s stance on Iran highlighted what Vatican officials have described as an unprecedented low in relations with the United States.

dailyNews.title: Poll finds broad rejection of religion-related messages from Trump, Hegseth
dailyNews.description: Americans rate Pope Leo positively but are deeply critical of the president’s social media post that appeared to depict him as Jesus, the Post-ABC-Ipsos poll found.

dailyNews.title: After defying Trump, a Republican lawmaker hangs on by a thread
dailyNews.description: Republicans in Indiana’s Senate tested voters’ tolerance for defying the president. Most lost in primaries amid a wave of threats, campaign cash and popular backlash.

dailyNews.title: 4 takeaways from the Indiana and Ohio primaries
dailyNews.description: Primaries in Indiana and Ohio reinforced President Donald Trump’s power in the GOP and set the stakes for several top-tier midterm races.
